description: Adds one or more tags to a run. Runs can have a maximum of 10 tags. Duplicate tags are ignored.

parameters.name: runId
parameters.description: The ID of an run, starts with `run_`. The run ID will be returned when you trigger a run on a task.

parameters.name: _body
parameters.description: JSON request body. Pass its fields directly inside `params`.

oneOf.description: A single run tag. Must be less than 128 characters.

items.description: A single run tag. Must be less than 128 characters.

tags.description: One or more tags to attach to a run. Runs can have a maximum of 10 tags.

searchText: add_run_tags_v1 add tags to a run adds one or more tags to a run. runs can have a maximum of 10 tags. duplicate tags are ignored. runs post /api/v1/runs/{runid}/tags runid _body tags

description: Batch trigger a specific task with up to 1,000 payloads. All items in the batch run the same task.

parameters.name: taskIdentifier
parameters.description: The id of a task

parameters.name: _body
parameters.description: JSON request body. Pass its fields directly inside `params`.

items.description: An array of payloads to trigger the task with (max 1,000 items).

payload.description: The payload can include any valid JSON

context.description: The context can include any valid JSON

name.description: You can define a shared queue and then pass the name in to your task.

concurrencyLimit.description: An optional property that specifies the maximum number of concurrent run executions. If this property is omitted, the task can potentially use up the full concurrency of an environment.

concurrencyKey.description: Scope the concurrency limit to a specific key.

idempotencyKey.description: An optional property that specifies the idempotency key used to prevent creating duplicate runs. If you provide an existing idempotency key, we will return the existing run ID.

ttl.description: The time-to-live for this run. If the run is not executed within this time, it will be removed from the queue and never execute. You can use a string in this format: `1h`, `1m`, `1h42m` or a number of seconds (min. 1).

delay.description:
The delay before the task is executed. This can be a Date object, a string like `1h` or a date-time string.

* "1h" - 1 hour
* "30d" - 30 days
* "15m" - 15 minutes
* "2w" - 2 weeks
* "60s" - 60 seconds
* new Date("2025-01-01T00:00:00Z")

oneOf.description: A single run tag. Must be less than 128 characters.

items.description: A single run tag. Must be less than 128 characters.

allOf.description: One or more tags to attach to a run. Runs can have a maximum of 10 tags.

tags.description:
Tags to attach to the run. Tags can be used to filter runs in the dashboard and using the SDK.

You can set up to 10 tags per run, each must be less than 128 characters.

We recommend prefixing tags with a namespace using an underscore or colon, like `user_1234567` or `org:9876543`. Stripe uses underscores.

machine.description: The machine preset to use for this run. This will override the task's machine preset and any defaults.

searchText: batch_trigger_task_by_id_v1 batch trigger a specific task batch trigger a specific task with up to 1,000 payloads. all items in the batch run the same task. tasks post /api/v1/tasks/{taskidentifier}/batch taskidentifier _body items payload context options queue name concurrencylimit concurrencykey idempotencykey ttl delay tags machine

description: Cancels an in-progress run. If the run is already completed, this will have no effect.

parameters.name: runId
parameters.description: The ID of an run, starts with `run_`. The run ID will be returned when you trigger a run on a task.

searchText: cancel_run_v1 cancel a run cancels an in-progress run. if the run is already completed, this will have no effect. runs post /api/v2/runs/{runid}/cancel runid

summary: Complete a waitpoint token via HTTP callback
description:
Completes a waitpoint token using the pre-signed callback URL returned in the `url` field when the token was created. No API key is required — the `callbackHash` in the URL acts as the authentication token.

This is designed to be given directly to external services (e.g. as a webhook URL) so they can unblock a waiting run without needing access to your API key. The entire request body is passed as the output data to the waiting run.

If the token is already completed, this is a no-op and returns `success: true`.

parameters.name: waitpointId
parameters.description: The ID of the waitpoint token.

parameters.name: callbackHash
parameters.description: The HMAC hash that authenticates the request. This is embedded in the `url` returned when creating the token — do not construct it manually.

parameters.name: _body
parameters.description: JSON request body. Pass its fields directly inside `params`.

schema.description: Any JSON object. The entire body is passed as the output data to the run waiting on this token. If the body is not valid JSON, an empty object is used.

searchText: complete_waitpoint_token_callback_v1 complete a waitpoint token via http callback completes a waitpoint token using the pre-signed callback url returned in the `url` field when the token was created. no api key is required — the `callbackhash` in the url acts as the authentication token. this is designed to be given directly to external services (e.g. as a webhook url) so they can unblock a waiting run without needing access to your api key. the entire request body is passed as the output data to the waiting run. if the token is already completed, this is a no-op and returns `success: true`. waitpoints post /api/v1/waitpoints/tokens/{waitpointid}/callback/{callbackhash} waitpointid callbackhash _body

description:
Completes a waitpoint token, unblocking any run that is waiting for it via `wait.forToken()`. An optional `data` payload can be passed and will be returned to the waiting run. If the token is already completed, this is a no-op and returns `success: true`.

This endpoint accepts both secret API keys and short-lived JWTs (public access tokens), making it safe to call from frontend clients.

parameters.name: waitpointId
parameters.description: The ID of the waitpoint token to complete.

parameters.name: _body
parameters.description: JSON request body. Pass its fields directly inside `params`.

data.description: Any JSON-serializable value to pass back to the run waiting on this token. The data will be returned from `wait.forToken()` as the result payload.

searchText: complete_waitpoint_token_v1 complete a waitpoint token completes a waitpoint token, unblocking any run that is waiting for it via `wait.fortoken()`. an optional `data` payload can be passed and will be returned to the waiting run. if the token is already completed, this is a no-op and returns `success: true`. this endpoint accepts both secret api keys and short-lived jwts (public access tokens), making it safe to call from frontend clients. waitpoints post /api/v1/waitpoints/tokens/{waitpointid}/complete waitpointid _body data

description: Create a new `IMPERATIVE` schedule based on the specified options.

parameters.name: _body
parameters.description: JSON request body. Pass its fields directly inside `params`.

timezone.description: Defaults to "UTC". In IANA format ("America/New_York"). If set then it will trigger at the CRON frequency in that timezone and respect daylight savings time.

searchText: create_schedule_v1 create a schedule create a new `imperative` schedule based on the specified options. schedules post /api/v1/schedules _body task cron deduplicationkey externalid timezone

description: Creates a new waitpoint token that can be used to pause a run until an external event completes it. The token includes a `url` which can be called via HTTP POST to complete the waitpoint. Use the token ID with `wait.forToken()` inside a task to pause execution until the token is completed.

parameters.name: _body
parameters.description: JSON request body. Pass its fields directly inside `params`.

idempotencyKey.description: An optional idempotency key. If you pass the same key twice before it expires, you will receive the original token back. The returned token may already be completed, in which case `wait.forToken()` will continue immediately.

idempotencyKeyTTL.description: How long the idempotency key is valid, after which passing the same key creates a new waitpoint. Accepts durations like "30s", "1m", "2h", "3d".

timeout.description: How long to wait before the token times out. When a run is waiting for a timed-out token, `wait.forToken()` returns with `ok: false`. Accepts an ISO 8601 date string or duration shorthand like "30s", "1m", "2h", "3d", "4w".

tags.description: Tags to attach to the waitpoint. You can set up to 10 tags, each under 128 characters. We recommend namespacing tags with a prefix like `user:1234567` or `org_9876543`.

searchText: create_waitpoint_token_v1 create a waitpoint token creates a new waitpoint token that can be used to pause a run until an external event completes it. the token includes a `url` which can be called via http post to complete the waitpoint. use the token id with `wait.fortoken()` inside a task to pause execution until the token is completed. waitpoints post /api/v1/waitpoints/tokens _body idempotencykey idempotencykeyttl timeout tags

description: Execute a TRQL (Trigger.dev Query Language) query against your run data. TRQL is a SQL-style query language that allows you to analyze runs, calculate metrics, and export data.

parameters.name: _body
parameters.description: JSON request body. Pass its fields directly inside `params`.

query.example: SELECT run_id, status, triggered_at FROM runs WHERE status = 'Failed' LIMIT 10

scope.description: The scope of data to query - environment (default), project, or organization

period.description: Time period shorthand (e.g., "7d", "30d", "1h"). Cannot be used with from/to.

from.description: Start of time range as ISO 8601 timestamp. Must be used with 'to'.

to.description: End of time range as ISO 8601 timestamp. Must be used with 'from'.

format.description: Response format - "json" returns structured data (default), "csv" returns CSV string

description: List runs in a project, across multiple environments, using Personal Access Token auth. You can filter the runs by status, created at, task identifier, version, and more.

parameters.name: projectRef
parameters.description: The external ref of the project. You can find this in the project settings. Starts with `proj_`.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description:
Use this parameter to paginate the results. You can specify the number of runs per page, and the ID of the run to start the page after or before.

For object fields like `page`, you should use the "form" encoding style. For example, to get the next page of runs, you can use `page[after]=run_1234`.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Use this parameter to filter the runs. You can filter by created at, environment, status, task identifier, and version.

For array fields, you can provide multiple values to filter by using a comma-separated list. For example, to get QUEUED and EXECUTING runs, you can use `filter[status]=QUEUED,EXECUTING`.

For object fields, you should use the "form" encoding style. For example, to filter by the period, you can use `filter[createdAt][period]=1d`.

searchText: list_project_runs_v1 list project runs list runs in a project, across multiple environments, using personal access token auth. you can filter the runs by status, created at, task identifier, version, and more. runs get /api/v1/projects/{projectref}/runs projectref page filter

description: Returns a paginated list of waitpoint tokens for the current environment. Results are ordered by creation date, newest first. Use cursor-based pagination with `page[after]` and `page[before]` to navigate pages.

parameters.name: page[size]
parameters.description: Number of tokens to return per page (1–100).

parameters.name: page[after]
parameters.description: Return tokens after this cursor (from `pagination.next` in a previous response).

parameters.name: page[before]
parameters.description: Return tokens before this cursor (from `pagination.previous` in a previous response).

parameters.name: filter[status]
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of statuses to filter by. Allowed values: `WAITING`, `COMPLETED`, `TIMED_OUT`.

parameters.name: filter[idempotencyKey]
parameters.description: Filter by idempotency key.

parameters.name: filter[tags]
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of tags to filter by.

parameters.name: filter[createdAt][period]
parameters.description: Shorthand time period to filter by creation date (e.g. `1h`, `24h`, `7d`). Cannot be combined with `filter[createdAt][from]` or `filter[createdAt][to]`.

parameters.name: filter[createdAt][from]
parameters.description: Filter tokens created at or after this ISO 8601 timestamp.

parameters.name: filter[createdAt][to]
parameters.description: Filter tokens created at or before this ISO 8601 timestamp.

searchText: list_waitpoint_tokens_v1 list waitpoint tokens returns a paginated list of waitpoint tokens for the current environment. results are ordered by creation date, newest first. use cursor-based pagination with `page[after]` and `page[before]` to navigate pages. waitpoints get /api/v1/waitpoints/tokens page[size] page[after] page[before] filter[status] filter[idempotencykey] filter[tags] filter[createdat][period] filter[createdat][from] filter[createdat][to]

description: Override the concurrency limit of a queue. This is useful for temporarily scaling up or down based on demand.

parameters.name: queueParam

parameters.name: _body
parameters.description: JSON request body. Pass its fields directly inside `params`.

type.description:
How to interpret the `queueParam` path parameter:
- `id`: Treat as a queue ID (default)
- `task`: Treat as a task ID to get the task's default queue
- `custom`: Treat as a custom queue name

concurrencyLimit.description: The new concurrency limit to set for the queue

How to interpret the `queueParam` path parameter:
- `id`: Treat as a queue ID (default)

description: Retrieve a batch by its ID, including its status and the IDs of all runs in the batch.

parameters.name: batchId
parameters.description: The ID of the batch, starts with `batch_`.

searchText: retrieve_batch_v1 retrieve a batch retrieve a batch by its id, including its status and the ids of all runs in the batch. batches get /api/v1/batches/{batchid} batchid

description: Get a queue by its ID, or by type and name.

parameters.name: queueParam
parameters.description: The queue ID (e.g., `queue_1234`), or the name of the queue when using the `type` query parameter.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
How to interpret the `queueParam` path parameter:
- `id`: Treat as a queue ID (default)
- `task`: Treat as a task ID to get the task's default queue
- `custom`: Treat as a custom queue name

searchText: retrieve_queue_v1 retrieve a queue get a queue by its id, or by type and name. queues get /api/v1/queues/{queueparam} queueparam type

description: Retrieve information about a run, including its status, payload, output, and attempts. If you authenticate with a Public API key, we will omit the payload and output fields for security reasons.

parameters.name: runId
parameters.description: The ID of an run, starts with `run_`. The run ID will be returned when you trigger a run on a task.

searchText: retrieve_run_v1 retrieve a run retrieve information about a run, including its status, payload, output, and attempts. if you authenticate with a public api key, we will omit the payload and output fields for security reasons. run get /api/v3/runs/{runid} runid

description: Retrieves a waitpoint token by its ID, including its current status and output if it has been completed.

parameters.name: waitpointId
parameters.description: The ID of the waitpoint token.

searchText: retrieve_waitpoint_token_v1 retrieve a waitpoint token retrieves a waitpoint token by its id, including its current status and output if it has been completed. waitpoints get /api/v1/waitpoints/tokens/{waitpointid} waitpointid

description: Trigger a task by its identifier.

parameters.name: taskIdentifier
parameters.description: The id of a task

parameters.name: _body
parameters.description: JSON request body. Pass its fields directly inside `params`.

payload.description: The payload can include any valid JSON

context.description: The context can include any valid JSON

name.description: You can define a shared queue and then pass the name in to your task.

concurrencyLimit.description: An optional property that specifies the maximum number of concurrent run executions. If this property is omitted, the task can potentially use up the full concurrency of an environment.

concurrencyKey.description: Scope the concurrency limit to a specific key.

idempotencyKey.description: An optional property that specifies the idempotency key used to prevent creating duplicate runs. If you provide an existing idempotency key, we will return the existing run ID.

ttl.description: The time-to-live for this run. If the run is not executed within this time, it will be removed from the queue and never execute. You can use a string in this format: `1h`, `1m`, `1h42m` or a number of seconds (min. 1).

delay.description:
The delay before the task is executed. This can be a Date object, a string like `1h` or a date-time string.

* "1h" - 1 hour
* "30d" - 30 days
* "15m" - 15 minutes
* "2w" - 2 weeks
* "60s" - 60 seconds
* new Date("2025-01-01T00:00:00Z")

oneOf.description: A single run tag. Must be less than 128 characters.

items.description: A single run tag. Must be less than 128 characters.

allOf.description: One or more tags to attach to a run. Runs can have a maximum of 10 tags.

tags.description:
Tags to attach to the run. Tags can be used to filter runs in the dashboard and using the SDK.

You can set up to 10 tags per run, each must be less than 128 characters.

We recommend prefixing tags with a namespace using an underscore or colon, like `user_1234567` or `org:9876543`. Stripe uses underscores.

machine.description: The machine preset to use for this run. This will override the task's machine preset and any defaults.

searchText: trigger_task_v1 trigger a task trigger a task by its identifier. tasks post /api/v1/tasks/{taskidentifier}/trigger taskidentifier _body payload context options queue name concurrencylimit concurrencykey idempotencykey ttl delay tags machine

description: Update a specific environment variable for a specific project and environment.

parameters.name: projectRef
parameters.description: The external ref of the project. You can find this in the project settings. Starts with `proj_`.

parameters.name: env
parameters.description: The environment of the project to list variables for.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the environment variable.

parameters.name: _body
parameters.description: JSON request body. Pass its fields directly inside `params`.

searchText: update_project_envvar_v1 update environment variable update a specific environment variable for a specific project and environment. envvars put /api/v1/projects/{projectref}/envvars/{env}/{name} projectref env name _body value

description: Update the metadata of a run.

parameters.name: runId
parameters.description: The ID of an run, starts with `run_`. The run ID will be returned when you trigger a run on a task.

parameters.name: _body
parameters.description: JSON request body. Pass its fields directly inside `params`.

metadata.description: The new metadata to set on the run.

searchText: update_run_metadata_v1 update run metadata update the metadata of a run. runs put /api/v1/runs/{runid}/metadata runid _body metadata

description: Update a schedule by its ID. This will only work on `IMPERATIVE` schedules that were created in the dashboard or using the imperative SDK functions like `schedules.create()`.

parameters.name: schedule_id
parameters.description: The ID of the schedule.

parameters.name: _body
parameters.description: JSON request body. Pass its fields directly inside `params`.

timezone.description: Defaults to "UTC". In IANA format ("America/New_York"). If set then it will trigger at the CRON frequency in that timezone and respect daylight savings time.

searchText: update_schedule_v1 update schedule update a schedule by its id. this will only work on `imperative` schedules that were created in the dashboard or using the imperative sdk functions like `schedules.create()`. schedules put /api/v1/schedules/{schedule_id} schedule_id _body task cron externalid timezone

description: Upload mulitple environment variables for a specific project and environment.

parameters.name: projectRef
parameters.description: The external ref of the project. You can find this in the project settings. Starts with `proj_`.

parameters.name: env
parameters.description: The environment of the project to list variables for.

parameters.name: _body
parameters.description: JSON request body. Pass its fields directly inside `params`.

override.description: Whether to override existing variables or not

searchText: upload_project_envvars_v1 upload environment variables upload mulitple environment variables for a specific project and environment. envvars post /api/v1/projects/{projectref}/envvars/{env}/import projectref env _body variables name value override
